what: Two SwiftIS-Prod certificates expire within 2 days of each other - OAuth ServiceKey on March 20 and Cloud Connector X509 on March 22
why: Simultaneous expiry creates a single outage window that takes down both cloud integration flows and on-premise S4H connectivity at the same time. Missing one renewal blocks finance and HR flows.
action: Renew both certs in one maintenance window this week. Prioritise OAuth ServiceKey first. Enable auto-renew on both to prevent recurrence.

what: BDC_DS_US_Prod Datasphere space is at 95% utilization. Daily DBX-US-Sandbox ingestion jobs are running during business hours driving the spike.
why: At current growth rate US Prod hits the hard limit in 4-6 days. When full Datasphere jobs fail silently and SAC-US-Prod live report refreshes stop working. BDC_DS_Global_Prod at 93.7% is next.
action: Throttle DBX-US-Sandbox jobs to off-peak hours (10pm-6am). Request 100GB space increase for US Prod this week. Set alert threshold to 85% for earlier warning.

what: SwiftIS-Prod Event Mesh queue is at 97% partition capacity. Finance integration messages between S4H and Integration Suite are backing up.
why: At 100% the queue drops new messages. Finance posting flows that rely on Event Mesh will silently lose transactions - a data integrity risk that is difficult to reconcile after the fact.
action: Increase EventMesh partition count 3x immediately. Add dead-letter queue for overflow detection. Coordinate with S4H team to throttle high-volume posting batches.

what: DBX-US-Sandbox ingestion jobs migrated from off-peak to daytime slots causing 17.5% cost overrun on BDC_DS_US_Prod and capacity pressure simultaneously
why: The POC was scoped as overnight testing only. Daytime jobs compete with production Datasphere queries inflating CU consumption above the $2000 monthly budget and accelerating the capacity risk.
action: Reschedule all DBX-US-Sandbox jobs to 10pm-6am EST. Add CU consumption tags to Databricks jobs for accurate cost attribution. Review POC scope before connecting DBX-Global-Sandbox.

what: Open Connectors idle 45 days ($18/month) and BPA CF runtime idle 22 days ($125/month) - combined $143/month with zero business value
why: Both services were part of projects that have since paused or completed. No restart dates confirmed. Budget is being consumed by idle infrastructure every billing cycle.
action: Decommission Open Connectors or assign to a new project within 2 weeks. Confirm BPA project status with project owner - if restart beyond 30 days suspend the CF runtime to stop compute billing.

what: Joule-Prod has 33 unused Premium seats (56% utilization) at $22/seat - $726/month waste. Seats were provisioned for the planned Global Financial AI use case not yet started.
why: Premium seats are sitting idle while the Global finance project is still in planning. The US POC team using the same pool creates ambiguity in usage attribution and inflates reported consumption.
action: Downgrade 33 unused Premium seats to Base tier until Global Financial use case is formally scoped and approved. Re-provision as Premium when project kicks off. Estimated saving $726/month.

what: BDC_DS_Global_Dev has 22GB headroom before DBX-Global-Sandbox connection is established. Once Global Databricks ingestion begins it will follow the same pattern as US Dev.
why: If DBX-Global connects without a sizing review it will replicate the US Dev situation where the space is already at 84% driven by POC ingestion. Better to size proactively than reactively.
action: Review planned data volumes for DBX-Global-Sandbox before provisioning the destination connection. Size Global Dev space based on actual Databricks dataset size not by matching US Dev allocation.

what: SwiftIS-Stage and SwiftIS-QA are running at 42% and 52% of budget with no major testing cycles planned this month. Combined unused headroom of ~$670.
why: Budget was set at the start of the quarter based on projected sprint activity that has since been deferred. Reallocating to environments with active overages is more efficient.
action: Reallocate $400 from Stage and QA budgets to SwiftIS-Prod and BDC_DS_US_Prod where overages are occurring. Review environment budgets quarterly aligned to the release calendar.
